data.title: Streak CRM: Manage Your Entire Sales Pipeline Inside Gmail
data.excerpt: Discover how Streak CRM turns Gmail into a powerful sales platform. Track deals, automate follow-ups, and close more without leaving your inbox.

$media.alt: Streak CRM: Manage Your Entire Sales Pipeline Inside Gmail

children.text: If your sales process lives in your inbox but your CRM lives somewhere else, you're doing double the work. Every lead you email, every follow-up you send, every deal you track — it's all happening in Gmail already. So why not manage it there too?
That's exactly what
Streak

does. It transforms Gmail into a fully functional CRM without requiring you to open a new tab, learn a new interface, or migrate your data anywhere. Pipelines, mail merges, email tracking, task reminders — all embedded directly into the tool you already use 4+ hours a day.

Trusted by over 750,000 professionals and companies like Uber, Spotify, and Harvard, Streak isn't some lightweight add-on. It's a serious sales infrastructure that happens to live where your conversations already do.
What Exactly Is Streak?

Streak is a CRM platform built specifically for Google Workspace. Once installed as a browser extension (it takes about 30 seconds), it adds a suite of sales and relationship management tools directly inside Gmail.

Here's what makes it different from traditional CRMs like Salesforce, HubSpot, or Pipedrive: there's no separate app to log into. No syncing issues between your email client and your CRM. No copy-pasting contact info from one system to another. Everything is contextual — when you open an email from a prospect, you see their deal stage, recent notes, tasks, and full interaction history right next to the conversation.

The result? You actually use your CRM. Adoption rates for Streak are remarkably high compared to standalone platforms, and that's not a coincidence. When the tool lives where you already work, resistance disappears.
Core Features That Make Streak Shine
Streak packs a surprising amount of functionality into its Gmail integration. Here's what you get out of the box:
Pipelines

Streak's pipeline system is the heart of the platform. You can create custom pipelines for any workflow — sales, recruiting, fundraising, support tickets, content production — and track items through stages with drag-and-drop simplicity. Each box (deal, candidate, ticket) has its own page with fields you define, notes, tasks, and linked emails.

The pipeline view gives you a visual Kanban-style board where you can see your entire workflow at a glance. It's incredibly satisfying to drag a deal from "Proposal Sent" to "Closed Won" and watch your metrics update in real time.
Mail Merge
Need to send personalized emails to 50, 200, or 1,000 prospects? Streak's
mail merge

handles it beautifully. You build your email template with merge fields pulled from your pipeline data, schedule sends, and track opens and clicks. It feels like sending a personal email, but at scale.

Unlike standalone mail merge tools, Streak's version is deeply integrated with your pipeline. So when a recipient replies, the email automatically lands in the right deal's history. No manual tagging required.
Email Tracking

Know the moment someone opens your email or clicks a link. Streak's tracking is real-time and surprisingly accurate. You get desktop notifications, so you can follow up while the conversation is fresh in your prospect's mind. That kind of timing makes a real difference in close rates.
Task Management

Streak lets you create tasks tied to specific deals or contacts, with due dates and reminders. Forget about maintaining a separate to-do app for your sales follow-ups — they live right alongside the deals they belong to.
Snippets

Save your most-used email templates as snippets and insert them with a keyboard shortcut. Perfect for common responses, follow-up sequences, or proposal introductions. It's a small feature that saves hours over time.
Team Collaboration

Shared pipelines, shared email threads, and team-wide reporting mean your entire sales team operates from a single source of truth. No more "who's working this lead?" Slack messages. Everyone sees the same pipeline with the same data.
Want to learn the practical steps for setting all this up? Check out our
step-by-step Streak tutorial
New & Highlighted Features
Streak continues to evolve, and some recent additions deserve your attention:
AI Credits

Streak now includes AI credits in every paid plan (20 in Pro, 150 in Pro+, 500 in Enterprise). These credits power intelligent features like automated email drafting, deal summaries, and smart suggestions based on your pipeline data. It's like having a sales assistant living inside your inbox.

For teams handling high email volume, the AI alone can reclaim hours each week. Imagine drafting a personalized follow-up in seconds instead of minutes, with context pulled directly from your previous conversations.
Advanced Reports (Pro+)

The reporting suite in Pro+ goes deep. Pipeline velocity, conversion rates by stage, team performance dashboards, activity metrics — it's the kind of analytics that usually requires a separate BI tool, built right into your CRM. If you're data-driven about your sales process, this is where Streak
really proves its value
Integrations & Automations (Pro+)

Streak connects with tools like Zapier, Slack, and Calendly, letting you build workflows that trigger automatically. New lead comes in from your website form? It's already in your pipeline. Deal moves to "Closed Won"? Your team gets a Slack notification. These automations eliminate the manual busywork that slows sales teams down.
Data Validation (Enterprise)

For larger teams, data quality is a constant battle. Streak's Enterprise plan adds validation rules that prevent reps from saving incomplete or incorrectly formatted records. Clean data in, clean reports out.
Custom Roles (Enterprise)

Control who sees what with granular permission settings. Managers get full visibility, reps see only their own deals, and sensitive data stays protected. It's the kind of access control that makes IT departments breathe easier.
Curious how Streak compares to other tools in your stack? We break it down in our
use cases guide
Who Is Streak Ideal For?
Streak isn't trying to be everything to everyone, and that focus is what makes it excellent. Here's who gets the most value:
Small to mid-size sales teams

who need a CRM but dread the onboarding process. If your team has resisted adopting Salesforce or HubSpot because "it's too complicated," Streak removes that friction entirely. The learning curve is nearly flat — it's Gmail with superpowers.
Solo founders and solopreneurs
who manage their own sales. When you're wearing every hat, you can't afford to waste time context-switching between tools. Streak keeps your pipeline, your emails, and your tasks in one place.
Freelancers and consultants
tracking client relationships and project proposals. Streak's flexible pipeline system works just as well for managing creative projects as it does for B2B sales.
Recruiters and hiring managers
who need to track candidates through interview stages. The pipeline metaphor translates perfectly to recruitment workflows.
Fundraising teams

at startups managing investor conversations. Many Y Combinator companies use Streak specifically for this purpose — it's simple enough to set up before a fundraise and powerful enough to track dozens of investor relationships simultaneously.
Any Google Workspace team
that's already living in Gmail. If your company runs on Google, Streak is the most natural CRM choice available. There's
nothing else quite like it
in terms of native integration depth.
Pricing: Straightforward & Fair
Streak offers three paid tiers with no hidden fees:
| Plan | Monthly | Annual (20% off) | Best For |
Pro
| $59/user/mo | $49/user/mo | Teams wanting core CRM + mail merge |
Pro+
| $89/user/mo | $69/user/mo | Teams needing automations & reports |
Enterprise
| $159/user/mo | $129/user/mo | Large teams requiring roles & validation |

The Pro plan covers the essentials most small teams need: full CRM, mail merge, shared pipelines, and 20 AI credits per user monthly. For teams that want to automate workflows and access detailed analytics, Pro+ is the sweet spot. Enterprise adds governance features that larger organizations require.
There's also a free Solo plan for individual users who want to try the basics before committing. And every paid plan comes with a 14-day free trial — no credit card required.
Compared to standalone CRMs that charge similar prices but require separate email tools, Streak's value proposition is strong. You're consolidating two tools into one.
Ready to see it in action?
Try Streak free for 14 days
and experience what a CRM feels like when it actually fits your workflow.
Why I Recommend Streak

I've tested dozens of CRMs over the years — the big names, the scrappy startups, the niche players. Most of them solve the same problems with slight variations in UX. Streak solves the core adoption problem that plagues every other CRM: getting people to actually use it.

When your CRM is a tab you have to open, you'll open it when you remember. When it's baked into the tool you never close, it becomes invisible in the best way. Your pipeline updates happen naturally as you work. Your follow-ups get logged automatically. Your reports compile themselves.

Streak also scales better than people expect. It starts simple enough for a solo user, but the Enterprise features — custom roles, data validation, dedicated support — make it viable for teams of 100+. That's rare for a Gmail-native tool.
The
Pro+ plan at $69/user/month

is where most growing teams will land, and it delivers genuinely powerful features. The automations alone justify the cost by eliminating manual data entry and ensuring nothing falls through the cracks.
If your team uses Gmail and you need a CRM — or if you have a CRM that nobody actually updates — give Streak a serious look.
Start your free trial here
and see the difference it makes when your CRM finally works where you work.

children.text: Is Streak CRM really free?

Streak offers a free Solo plan for individual users with basic pipeline and email tracking features. For team features like shared pipelines, mail merge, and advanced reporting, paid plans start at $49/user/month (billed annually). Every paid plan includes a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.
Does Streak work with Outlook or only Gmail?

Streak is built exclusively for Google Workspace and Gmail. It integrates as a browser extension and works natively within the Gmail interface. If your team uses Outlook, you'd need to consider alternative CRMs like HubSpot or Salesforce that support both platforms.
How does Streak compare to HubSpot CRM?

The biggest difference is where you work. HubSpot is a standalone platform you log into separately; Streak lives directly inside Gmail. For teams already using Google Workspace, Streak eliminates the context-switching that leads to low CRM adoption. HubSpot offers more marketing automation features out of the box, but Streak's Gmail integration depth and simplicity make it the stronger choice for teams that prioritize ease of use and seamless email workflow.
Can I import my existing contacts and deals into Streak?

Yes. Streak supports CSV imports for contacts and pipeline data, and it automatically pulls in your existing Gmail contacts. You can also connect it to other tools via Zapier or their API to migrate data from CRMs like Salesforce, HubSpot, or Pipedrive.
